pattern: Scan the string to isolate maximal digit runs, normalize each by stripping leading zeros, and count the distinct numeric values in a set.
hints: Every non-digit character acts as a separator between candidate integers.
Two digit runs represent the same integer only after their leading zeros are removed, so compare the normalized form.
Walk the string collecting contiguous digit substrings; strip leading zeros from each and insert the result into a set, then return the set size.

edge_inputs.description: letters and digits interleaved with a repeated number
different leading-zero forms are the same integer
only letters, no integers at all
all zeros collapse to one value
digits at both ends touching letters
long zero-padded number equals its trimmed form

stress.description: random mix of digits (incl. zeros) and letters to stress separation and normalization
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    chars = '0123456789abcdefghij'
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
